source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ FDA/참조 문서/3. Usability (IEC 62366)/Usability Validation Plan (Doc. No. UVP-01, Rev.0).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation plan according to IEC 62366 for conducting usability testing during development of the device identified as 제품명.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to plan usability testing, define participant responsibilities, test roles, usability metrics, test scenarios, acceptance criteria, usability goals, and reporting of usability verification and evaluation results.
keyPoints: The plan states that usability testing goals include establishing baseline user performance, validating user performance measures, and identifying potential design concerns.
A total of 3 participants should meet the stated user profile for the usability test.
Usability metrics include scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
Usability tasks include primary operating functions such as turning on the device, selecting X-ray exposure techniques, exposing the X-ray, and charging the battery.
The usability goal is a total completion rate of 100% for each usability task and total errors below 4 for each usability task.

concepts.name: Usability validation plan
concepts.evidence: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of 제품명.

concepts.name: Usability testing objectives
concepts.evidence: The usability test objectives are: To construct the benchmarks of user attributes; To identify potential design problems to be solve; To improve product design and production efficiency; To complete end-user satisfaction maximization

concepts.name: Usability test participants
concepts.evidence: A total 3 participants should be at least equal to the following User Profile used in the Usability test.

concepts.name: Usability metrics
concepts.evidence: Usability metrics refers to user performance measured against specific performance goals necessary to satisfy usability requirements.

concepts.name: Critical errors
concepts.evidence: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

concepts.name: Non-critical errors
concepts.evidence: Non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

concepts.name: Post-test satisfaction questionnaire
concepts.evidence: At the end of the test, participants will rate their satisfaction with the overall system.

concepts.name: Primary operating functions
concepts.evidence: Use scenarios related to the primary operating functions.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable worst case
concepts.evidence: Use scenarios related to the reasonably foreseeable worst case

concepts.name: Completion rate
concepts.evidence: Completion rate is the percentage of test participants who successfully complete the task without critical errors.

evidence.claim: The document is titled Usability Validation Plan.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Plan

evidence.claim: The document number is UVP-01.
evidence.evidenceText: Document Number UVP-01

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision History No. 1 Rev. No. 0 Description of changes Established by manufacturer’s Risk Management Policy Date 2016.07.31

evidence.claim: The preparation, review, and approval date shown in the document is 2018.02.10.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by Reviewed by Approved by ... Date 2018.02.10 2018.02.10 2018.02.10

evidence.claim: The plan is according to IEC 62366.
evidence.evidenceText: According to IEC 62366

evidence.claim: The document describes a test plan for conducting usability testing during product development.
evidence.evidenceText: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of 제품명.

evidence.claim: Usability testing goals include establishing baseline user performance, validating user performance measures, and identifying potential design concerns.
evidence.evidenceText: The goals of usability testing include establishing a baseline of user performance, establishing and validating user performance measures, and identifying potential design concerns to be addressed in order to improve the efficiency, productivity, and end-user satisfaction.

evidence.claim: The usability test uses a total of 3 participants.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 3 participants should be at least equal to the following User Profile used in the Usability test.

evidence.claim: Participants provide feedback on usability and acceptability and participate in questionnaires and debriefing.
evidence.evidenceText: The participants' responsibilities will be to attempt to complete a set of representative usability scenarios ... and to provide feedback regarding the usability and acceptability of the user interface.

evidence.claim: Participants receive an overview of the usability test procedure, equipment, and software.
evidence.evidenceText: The participants will receive and overview of the usability test procedure, equipment and software.

evidence.claim: The usability test occurs at the testing lab in a typical operating environment described in the usability engineering report.
evidence.evidenceText: Participants will take part in the usability test at the testing lab. The 제품명 will be used in a typical operating environment described in usability engineering report.

evidence.claim: Participants sign an informed consent acknowledging voluntary participation and that participation can cease at any time.
evidence.evidenceText: Participants will sign an informed consent that acknowledges: the participation is voluntary, that participation can cease at any time.

evidence.claim: Usability metrics include scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
evidence.evidenceText: Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, and subjective evaluations will be used. Time-to-completion of scenarios will also be collected.

evidence.claim: Critical errors include unresolved errors during task completion or errors producing an incorrect safety-related outcome.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

evidence.claim: Non-critical errors do not affect final task output but reduce task efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: Non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

evidence.claim: The post-test questionnaire uses a 5-point scale from 1 Strongly disagree to 5 Strongly agree.
evidence.evidenceText: The 5-point rating scale ranged from 1 (Strongly disagree) to 5 (Strongly agree)

evidence.claim: Primary operating function scenarios include turning on the device, X-ray exposure technique selection, exposing the X-ray, and charging the battery.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the primary operating functions. Turn on the device ... X-ray exposure technique selection ... Expose the x-ray ... Charge the battery

evidence.claim: Reasonably foreseeable worst-case scenarios include failures to understand the manual, position the product, turn on the product, check operation, clean, store, set X-ray conditions, and irradiate X-ray.
evidence.evidenceText: Use scenarios related to the reasonably foreseeable worst case ... User cannot turn on the product ... User does not know how to clean the device ... Radiation of incorrectly set X-rays happen because user does not know how to set the condition.

evidence.claim: The usability goal for completion rate is 100% for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: The usability goal for errors is below 4 total errors for each usability task.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total errors of below 4 is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: The usability verification and evaluation report is provided at the conclusion of the usability test.
evidence.evidenceText: The Usability verification & evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.

evidence.claim: The document references EN ISO 14971:2007 for risk management of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2007 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The document references EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 for usability as a collateral standard.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Usability

evidence.claim: The document references EN 62366:2008 for usability engineering of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366:2008 Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

reviewFlags.reason: Product Name and Model Name fields are present but blank in the source text; product model was not extracted.
Authority or jurisdiction is taken from source metadata authoritySignal and source path; the document text itself does not explicitly identify FDA as the issuing or governing authority.
Document date evidence is ambiguous: preparation/review/approval dates show 2018.02.10, while revision history for Rev.0 shows 2016.07.31.
The source text contains placeholder Korean product text 제품명 instead of a specific product name.
Several source phrases appear grammatically degraded or OCR/template-derived, such as repeated role text and acceptance criteria phrased as 'over 100% of operators'.
